issues.title: Waitlist workflow is described but not supported by tools
issues.why_it_matters: The prompt tells the agent to offer a waitlist and make a note, but no waitlist or note-writing tool exists in the tool schema.
issues.evidence_span: If after 3 searches they still have not found a time they like, offer to put them on a waitlist — take their preferred provider, date range, and time of day preference and make a note.
issues.failure_mode: The agent may claim to have completed a waitlist action that cannot actually be executed, which breaks trust and workflow integrity.
issues.recommended_fix: Replace waitlist promises with a supported fallback such as transferring to the front desk for manual follow-up, and explicitly forbid inventing unsupported note-taking actions.

issues.title: Scheduling tools require provider and location IDs, but prompt only talks about names
issues.why_it_matters: The prompt assumes the agent can schedule by human-readable provider and location names, but the tools require IDs and the prompt never defines how to obtain them.
issues.failure_mode: A real runtime can fail at the first slot search because the agent is missing required IDs and may start guessing.
issues.recommended_fix: Do not treat this as a prompt-only fix. Either add a real provider/location ID resolver to the runtime or route scheduling requests to a human workflow when IDs cannot be derived safely.

issues.title: Lab review rule depends on data the tools do not expose
issues.why_it_matters: The prompt requires booking lab reviews with the ordering provider, but the available appointment lookup data does not reveal who ordered the labs.
issues.evidence_span: Must be scheduled with the ordering provider only — do not schedule a lab review with a different doctor than the one who ordered the labs.
issues.failure_mode: The agent may confidently route a lab review to the wrong provider or ask unsupported follow-up questions that still do not resolve the ambiguity.
issues.recommended_fix: Tell the agent to ask the patient who ordered the labs, and if the answer is unknown or cannot be verified from tools, transfer instead of guessing.

issues.title: Human-readable appointment names do not map cleanly to tool enums
issues.why_it_matters: The prompt names appointment types in natural language, while the booking tools expect snake_case enum values.
issues.evidence_span:
Appointment types we offer:

One of: new_patient, follow_up, annual_physical, sick_visit, telehealth, procedure, lab_review, prenatal, well_child, urgent
issues.failure_mode: The agent can call tools with invalid or inconsistent appointment_type values, especially on a second unseen prompt.
issues.recommended_fix: Add a small normalization table that maps spoken appointment names to tool enum values before tool calls are made.

issues.title: Prenatal modality rule requires visit-history context not guaranteed by tools
issues.why_it_matters: The prompt says the first prenatal visit must be in person, but the available tool schema does not guarantee the agent can tell whether the caller has had a prior prenatal visit.
issues.evidence_span: First prenatal visit is always in-person, even if they request telehealth.
issues.failure_mode: The agent may misclassify follow-up prenatal telehealth requests or over-transfer because it lacks a reliable signal for visit sequence.
issues.recommended_fix: Tell the agent to ask whether this is the first prenatal visit and transfer if the answer remains uncertain rather than assuming.

issues.title: Prompt is long and repetitive enough to pressure conversational quality
issues.why_it_matters: Heavy repetition and many low-level speech rules can make the agent verbose, slower to decide, and more likely to dump policies instead of helping the caller.
issues.failure_mode: The agent may over-explain, restate policies unnecessarily, or miss the caller's emotional context because too many rules compete for attention.
issues.recommended_fix: Add a concise response-style rule that prioritizes short, task-first replies and avoiding repeated policy recitation unless it changes the decision.

issues.title: High-value workflow rules are scattered across the prompt
issues.why_it_matters: Policies are split across multiple reminder sections, which makes it harder for the analyzer and the runtime agent to find the governing rule quickly.
issues.evidence_span:
Some more things to remember:

Some additional scheduling guidelines:
issues.failure_mode: On an unseen prompt, the same concept may appear in several sections and the model may follow the wrong copy or miss a late exception.
issues.recommended_fix: Add a precedence reminder that later exceptions override general guidance and keep operational guardrails clustered near the workflows they affect.
